case_id: Aluminum, primary ingot, at plant (or similar primary aluminum process)

pi_items.verified_value: 2.0 kg CO2 eq/kg (verified for low-carbon recycled aluminum; primary aluminum typically higher)
pi_items.source: Hydro CIRCAL low-carbon aluminum is verified at 2.0 kg CO2e/kg by DNV certification; real-world low-carbon primary aluminum claims exist but are rare and typically higher. This value represents the claimed supplier's offering.

pi_items.verified_value: 4.0-5.0 kg CO2 eq/kg (consistent with hydropower-based US primary aluminum production)
pi_items.source: Multiple sources confirm that US primary aluminum with hydroelectric power achieves <4 kg CO2 eq/kg. International Aluminium Institute reports global range of <4 (hydro-based) to >20 (coal-based). RMI and Aluminum Association docs indicate US producers with renewable energy achieve 4-5 kg CO2 eq/kg range.

pi_items.verified_value: 20.0 kg CO2 eq/kg (consistent with upper bound of primary aluminum production with coal-based electricity)
pi_items.source: Research indicates coal-based regions can exceed 20 kg CO2 eq/kg. Global weighted average is 14.8-18.3 kg CO2 eq/kg. International Aluminium Institute statistics show range from <4 to >20 depending on energy source. 20.0 represents reasonable upper bound for consideration.

Task specifies REJECTED if US average is significantly different from 2.0 (outside 1.6-2.4 kg CO2 eq range). If expected_us_avg_min is 4.0 and expected_us_avg_max is 20.0, then 2.0 would indeed be outside the 20% tolerance of nearly all values in this range, making REJECTED the correct verdict.

pi_items.verified_value: TRACI 2.1 (Tool for the Reduction and Assessment of Chemicals and Other Environmental Impacts version 2.1)
pi_items.source: EPA official documentation confirms TRACI 2.1 is a real, well-established LCIA method. TRACI 2.1 includes Global Warming Potential (GWP) as core impact category with 100-year time horizon consistent with IPCC/UNFCCC guidance. Available in OpenLCA LCIA method packages.
Confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which reference this exact path. Directory structure is set up by scripts.

USLCI Winter 2025 release notes confirm aluminum beverage can processes including 'aluminum ingot/slab'. NREL USLCI database contains processes matching these keywords. LCA Commons repository documents aluminum ingot as standard process type.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real, publicly available data from two authoritative sources: the USLCI (U.S. Life Cycle Inventory) Database maintained by NREL, and the TRACI 2.1 LCIA method developed by the EPA. The supplier's claim of 2.0 kg CO2 eq/kg for aluminum is consistent with verified low-carbon aluminum products (Hydro CIRCAL at 2.0 kg CO2 eq/kg). The expected US primary aluminum range of 4.0-20.0 kg CO2 eq/kg is reasonable and consistent with published literature: hydropower-based US production achieves ~4 kg CO2 eq/kg, global average is 14.8-18.3 kg CO2 eq/kg, and coal-based production can exceed 20 kg CO2 eq/kg. The verdict of REJECTED is logically correct because 2.0 kg CO2 eq/kg falls outside the 20% tolerance band (1.6-2.4 range) of most expected US average values in the 4.0-20.0 range. TRACI 2.1 is a real EPA method that includes Global Warming Potential (GWP) measured in kg CO2-Eq with a 100-year characterization horizon.
